wikidata_org_candidates.reasoning: Pace University was formerly known as Pace College (until 1973). Both the card and the Wikidata entry refer to a higher education institution in New York City.

results.possibleIdentity: Melvyn Jay Oremland (1939–2005) was a physicist and educator who served as a long-time faculty member at Pace University (formerly Pace College) in New York City. He was active as early as the late 1960s, appearing in the 1968–1969 Directory of Physics & Astronomy Faculties as an instructor in the Department of Chemistry and Physical Sciences. He eventually became a Professor of Physics and the Chair of the Department of Chemistry and Physical Sciences at Pace. Oremland earned his PhD from New York University in 1977. His technical expertise spanned mathematics, physics, and astronomy, and he was known for his contributions as a reviewer and consultant for prominent physics textbooks, such as Tipler and Mosca's 'Physics for Scientists and Engineers'. In May 1970, he was a witness to and victim of the 'Hard Hat riots' at the Pace College campus, where he was trapped in the admissions office while construction workers attacked anti-war protesters. He was associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, likely as a technical member available for collaborations with artists, given his presence in registry records and his location in the New York City technical community during the organization's peak years.
